---
title: "A Quarto + Typst Research Poster"
subtitle: "Customizable and reusable poster workflow"
author:
  - name: "Ada Lovelace"
    affiliation: "Department of Computational Methods"
  - name: "Alan Turing"
    affiliation: "Institute for Mathematical Research"
format:
  research-poster-typst:
    poster-width: 48in
    poster-height: 36in
    poster-margin-x: 0.8in
    poster-margin-y: 0.6in
    poster-columns: 3
    poster-column-gap: 0.45in
    poster-column-widths: [1.1fr, 1fr, 0.9fr]
    poster-header-height: 16%
    poster-footer-height: 8%
    poster-primary: rgb("#1f3c88")
    poster-secondary: rgb("#00a6a6")
    poster-section-bg: rgb("#edf3ff")
    poster-footer-bg: rgb("#1f3c88")
    footer-left: "Scan for preprint"
    footer-center: "International Conference on Research Posters 2026"
    footer-right: "contact@lab.example"
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Background

This format is designed to reuse familiar Quarto metadata (`title`, `subtitle`, `author`, and affiliations) while providing poster-specific controls for layout, branding, and structure.

# Methods

- Poster dimensions, margins, and column settings are configurable in YAML.
- Header and footer regions are fixed layout zones with customizable size and colors.
- Section styling is controlled through theme keys.

# Results

You can structure each column naturally with Markdown headings and content blocks.

## Example subsection

The default style gives visual distinction across section levels while preserving readability at large poster scales.

# Conclusion

This extension is intended as a practical starting point for a reusable academic poster workflow using Quarto and Typst.
